review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for two SAM questions covering promptable segmentation and ambiguity handling.

question_bank.question: Why is promptable segmentation important in SAM?
choices: Because prompts help specify exactly which object or instance the user wants segmented
Because SAM can only segment images if the prompt is a full sentence caption
Because segmentation works only when the image contains one object category
Because promptable segmentation removes the need for an image encoder
question_bank.explanation: Segmentation requests are often ambiguous. A point, box, or other prompt tells the model which object or region the user actually cares about.

question_bank.question: Why does SAM output multiple masks for a single prompt?
choices: Because a prompt can correspond to multiple valid object extents or granularities
Because the decoder cannot produce a single mask at test time
Because SAM must always segment every object in the image simultaneously
Because mask diversity replaces the need for training data
question_bank.explanation: Even a simple point can be ambiguous about whether the user wants a part, an instance, or a larger region. Returning multiple candidates lets the system preserve those plausible interpretations.
